assertion_results.text: Sibling check correctly classifies TC-8001 as a different-stream companion (stream [rhtpa-2.2] vs current issue's [rhtpa-2.1]), not a same-stream duplicate (Step 4)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 18-21: 'TC-8006 stream suffix: [rhtpa-2.1] (stream 2.1.x)', 'TC-8001 stream suffix: [rhtpa-2.2] (stream 2.2.x)', 'Classification: Different-stream companion -- TC-8001 is scoped to stream 2.2.x while TC-8006 is scoped to stream 2.1.x. These are companion trackers for the same CVE across different streams, NOT same-stream duplicates.'

assertion_results.text: Step 4.2 checks the current issue's existing issuelinks before attempting to create a 'Related' link to TC-8001 (§1.58)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 29-38: 'Before attempting to create a "Related" link to sibling TC-8001, the current issue's existing issuelinks array (fetched in Step 1) was inspected.' followed by a table listing the existing link (ID 1990401, Type Related, outward TC-8006 -> TC-8001).

assertion_results.text: Step 4.2 detects the pre-existing 'Related' link to TC-8001 and skips link creation — does NOT call jira.create_link (§1.58)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md line 43: 'Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping link creation. The pre-existing Related link (ID: 1990401) already connects TC-8006 to TC-8001. Creating another link would be a duplicate, so jira.create_link is NOT called.'

assertion_results.text: The output includes a log message indicating the link was skipped because it already exists (e.g., 'Related link to TC-8001 already exists — skipping')
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md line 43: '**Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping link creation.**' and triage-outcome.md line 26: '**Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping link creation.**'

assertion_results.text: The sibling landscape table is still presented to the engineer despite the link already existing — the idempotent check only affects link creation, not the sibling summary
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 54-65: 'Despite the link already existing, the sibling landscape is presented to the engineer for context:' followed by the companion issues table showing TC-8001 (2.2.x, In Progress) and TC-8006 (2.1.x, New). triage-outcome.md lines 34-36 explicitly confirms: 'The idempotent check only affects link creation. The following outputs were still produced despite the link already existing: ... Sibling landscape table -- the companion issue summary was still presented to the engineer for context.'
